req.title: System-Admin updates org-announcement
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a org-announcement through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

tests.name: naive_case

doc._raw_response:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for requirement:
An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update an org-announcement
through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record
must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime, timezone


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def system_admin_user():
    """A fully authenticated system-admin user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 1
    user.username = "sys_admin"
    user.role = "system-admin"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def non_admin_user():
    """An authenticated user without system-admin privileges."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 2
    user.username = "regular_user"
    user.role = "viewer"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """A user that is NOT authenticated."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = None
    user.username = None
    user.role = None
    user.is_authenticated = False
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def existing_announcement():
    """A persisted org-announcement that will be updated."""
    announcement = MagicMock()
    announcement.id = 42
    announcement.title = "Original Title"
    announcement.body = "Original body text."
    announcement.org_id = 10
    announcement.is_active = True
    announcement.created_at = datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    announcement.updated_at = datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    return announcement


@pytest.fixture
def valid_form_data():
    """A complete, valid set of form data for updating an announcement."""
    return {
        "title": "Updated Announcement Title",
        "body": "Updated body with important information.",
        "org_id": 10,
        "is_active": True,
    }

        "is_active": True,
    }

    """Mocked announcement service layer."""
    service = MagicMock()

    service.update.return_value = existing_announcement

@pytest.fixture
def audit_service():
    """Mocked audit/logging service."""

@pytest.fixture
def ui_form_handler(announcement_service, audit_service):
    """
    The UI form handler (controller / view function) under test.
    Import or construct it here; in a real project this would be:
        from myapp.views import update_announcement_view
    For test isolation we define it inline so the tests remain self-contained.
    """
    from types import SimpleNamespace

    class UpdateAnnouncementFormHandler:
        """Thin wrapper that mimics what a real view/controller does."""

        MANDATORY_FIELDS = ("title", "body", "org_id")

        def __init__(self, ann_service, aud_service):
            self._ann_service = ann_service
            self._aud_service = aud_service

        def handle(self, current_user, announcement_id, form_data: dict):
            # --- Authentication & authorisation ---
            if not current_user.is_authenticated:
                return SimpleNamespace(status=401, errors=["Authentication required."], data=None)

            if current_user.role != "system-admin":
                return SimpleNamespace(status=403, errors=["Insufficient privileges."], data=None)

            # --- Existence check ---
            announcement = self._ann_service.get_by_id(announcement_id)
            if announcement is None:
                return SimpleNamespace(status=404, errors=["Announcement not found."], data=None)
